title: Repair Heim-PC Node runtime JIT crash
goal: Diagnose and repair the host-level Node/V8 JIT crash that makes `node -e`, `npm`, `npx`, `pnpm` and the npm Codex entrypoint abort with rc 133 unless `NODE_OPTIONS=--jitless` is used.

acceptance.assertion: `node -e 'console.log(1)'`, `npm --version`, `npx --version` and `pnpm --version` complete without rc 133 in a normal shell, or a precise upstream/runtime blocker is recorded.
The fix does not globally force `NODE_OPTIONS=--jitless` unless the trade-off is explicitly accepted and documented.
Codex wrapper behavior remains green: `codex --version` resolves to the intended standalone Codex binary or a documented fallback.

metadata.live_audit_finding: `codex --version` crashed through the shell PTY wrapper because `~/bin/codexi` prepended `~/.npm-global/bin` and invoked the npm Codex entrypoint; direct `~/.local/bin/codex --version` returned codex-cli 0.142.2, while normal Node/npm still abort with a V8 SetPermissions fatal error and rc 133.
